visa_types.name: Long Stay
visa_types.overview:
Long-Stay visa (D type) include all the categories of visa for a period of stay over 90 days: study, work, family reunion, religious, re-entry, etc.
- Study in Belgium (#studyinbelgium)
- Work in Belgium (#workinbelgium)
- Family Reunion (#familyreunion)
- Au pair (#aupair)
- Researcher (#researcher)
- Working Holiday (#workingholiday)
- Marriage in Belgium (#marriageinbelgium)
- Co-habitation in Belgium (#cohabitationinbelgium)
visa_types.full_text:
Long-Stay visa (D type) include all the categories of visa for a period of stay over 90 days: study, work, family reunion, religious, re-entry, etc.
- Study in Belgium (#studyinbelgium)
- Work in Belgium (#workinbelgium)
- Family Reunion (#familyreunion)
- Au pair (#aupair)
- Researcher (#researcher)
- Working Holiday (#workingholiday)
- Marriage in Belgium (#marriageinbelgium)
- Co-habitation in Belgium (#cohabitationinbelgium)

visa_types.name: Family and Friends Visit
visa_types.documents_required:
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for Visit Family and Friends visa.
Kindly click here to download checklist for Visiting Friends
Kindly click here to download checklist for visiting Family
visa_types.visa_fees:
[bel > nzl > en > Table:Family and Friends Visit > Visa Fees 1]
- Visa Service Fee for Auckland and Wellington : 80 Euros
- All fees to be paid in local currency as per prevailing currency exchange
visa_types.processing_time:
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/en).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If the purpose of traveling is to visit family or friends in Belgium you need to apply for Visiting Family & Friends Schengen visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If the purpose of traveling is to visit family or friends in Belgium you need to apply for Visiting Family & Friends Schengen visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)

## Visa Fees

[bel > nzl > en > Table:Family and Friends Visit > Visa Fees 1]
- Visa Service Fee for Auckland and Wellington : 80 Euros
- All fees to be paid in local currency as per prevailing currency exchange

## Documents Required
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for Visit Family and Friends visa.
Kindly click here to download checklist for Visiting Friends
Kindly click here to download checklist for visiting Family

## Photo Specifications
click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/en).

## Download Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=/en) to fill in the online application form

visa_types.visa_fees:
[bel > nzl > en > Table:Tourism > Visa Fees 1]

New visa fees for nationals of Cape Verde
reduced visa fees for nationals from Cape Verde applying for all short stay (Schengen) visas.
- Nationals of Cape Verde aged 18+: €60 = $96
- Nationals of Cape Verde between 12 and 18 years old: €30 = $48
- Nationals of Cape Verde under 12 years: free (0)

Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If the purpose of traveling is Tourism you need to apply for a Tourist Schengen visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)

## Visa Fees

[bel > nzl > en > Table:Tourism > Visa Fees 1]

New visa fees for nationals of Cape Verde
reduced visa fees for nationals from Cape Verde applying for all short stay (Schengen) visas.
- Nationals of Cape Verde aged 18+: €60 = $96
- Nationals of Cape Verde between 12 and 18 years old: €30 = $48
- Nationals of Cape Verde under 12 years: free (0)

## Documents Required
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for Tourism visa.
Kindly click here to download checklist

## Photo Specifications
click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/en).

## Download Form

visa_types.name: AU Pair
visa_types.visa_fees:
[bel > nzl > en > Table:AU Pair > Visa Fees 1]
- Visa Service Fee for Auckland and Wellington: 80 Euro
- All fees to be paid in local currency as per prevailing currency exchange
visa_types.processing_time:
Don’t wait until the last moment to  submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Foreign students wishing to work in Belgium as Au Pair need a visa accordingly.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- Foreign workers who wish to work in Belgium as employees (i.e. working under an employment contract) must hold a work permit, and the visa is needed accordingly.
- Foreign nationals wishing to settle in Belgium and be self-employed must hold a professional card authorising them to exercise their profession, and the visa is needed accordingly.
- For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels:
https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
- Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- Foreign workers who wish to work in Belgium as employees (i.e. working under an employment contract) must hold a work permit, and the visa is needed accordingly.
- Foreign nationals wishing to settle in Belgium and be self-employed must hold a professional card authorising them to exercise their profession, and the visa is needed accordingly.
- For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels:
https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
- Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

## Visa Fees

- All fees to be paid in local currency as per prevailing currency exchange

visa_types.full_text:
Short-Stay visa (C type) include all the categories of visa for a period of stay less than 90 days: Directive 2004-38-EC, etc.
- Directive  2004-38-EC (#directive2004-38-ec)

visa_types.name: Researcher
visa_types.visa_fees:
[bel > nzl > en > Table:Researcher > Visa Fees 1]
- Visa Service Fee for Auckland and Wellington: 80 Euro
- All fees to be paid in local currency as per prevailing currency exchange
visa_types.processing_time:
Don’t wait until the last moment to  submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Foreigners wishing to do research in  Belgium need  visas accordingly. 
For more information, please  visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
Applicants  applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear  in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which  includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Foreigners wishing to do research in  Belgium need  visas accordingly. 
For more information, please  visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
Applicants  applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear  in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which  includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

## Visa Fees

[bel > nzl > en > Table:Researcher > Visa Fees 1]
- Visa Service Fee for Auckland and Wellington: 80 Euro
- All fees to be paid in local currency as per prevailing currency exchange

## Documents Required
Checklist for Researchers

## Photo Specifications
click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Don’t wait until the last moment to  submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)

## Download Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen) to fill in the online application form

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Belgium and New Zealand signed an Agreement on a Working Holiday Scheme on 8 April 2003 in Brussels. This Agreement came into force on 1 November 2004. It aims at allowing young Belgians to go to New Zealand and young New Zealanders to come to Belgium, for a maximum period of one year. The main purpose of their journey should be for holiday while working is permitted as a secondary occupation to augment their financial resources.
For more information, please  visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Belgium and New Zealand signed an Agreement on a Working Holiday Scheme on 8 April 2003 in Brussels. This Agreement came into force on 1 November 2004. It aims at allowing young Belgians to go to New Zealand and young New Zealanders to come to Belgium, for a maximum period of one year. The main purpose of their journey should be for holiday while working is permitted as a secondary occupation to augment their financial resources.
For more information, please  visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)

## Visa Fees

[bel > nzl > en > Table:Working Holiday > Visa Fees 1]

- All fees to be paid in local currency as per prevailing currency exchange

Submit  a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If the purpose of traveling is to get married in Belgium you need to apply for Marry in Belgium Schengen visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If the purpose of traveling is to get married in Belgium you need to apply for Marry in Belgium Schengen visa. This service is available for residents who wish to apply for a Belgian Visa.

## Visa Fees

[bel > nzl > en > Table:Marriage in Belgium > Visa Fees 1]
- Visa Service Fee for Auckland and Wellington : 80 Euros
- All fees to be paid in local currency as per prevailing currency exchange

## Documents Required
To apply for a long term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for Marriage in Belgium visa.
Kindly click here to download checklist

## Photo Specifications
click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Don’t wait until the last moment to submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs. (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)

## Download Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=/en) to fill in the online application form

visa_types.processing_time:
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/en).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If the purpose of is to participate the professional training in Belgium, you need to apply for Professional Training Schengen visa.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If the purpose of is to participate the professional training in Belgium, you need to apply for Professional Training Schengen visa.
For more information, please visit the website of the Immigration Office in Brussels: https://dofi.ibz.be/en (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)

## Visa Fees

[bel > nzl > en > Table:Professional Training > Visa Fees 1]
- Visa Service Fee for Auckland and Wellington : 80 Euros
- All fees to be paid in local currency as per prevailing currency exchange

## Documents Required
To apply for a short term Schengen visa you will need multiple documents. Below are the requirements for Professional Training visa.
Kindly click here to download checklist

## Photo Specifications
click here to get the new photograph requirements.

## Processing Time
Applications received at the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General will be processed within 2 and 15 working days, provided that a complete file is submitted. We recommend that you introduce your applications 15 days prior to the intended departure date.
Applications can be introduced up to 90 days in advance.
In case the file has to be submitted for decision to the Immigration Office in Brussels, the processing time may take more than 15 days.
Passports are kept at the Embassy for the duration of the processing of the visa application. Should you have an urgent need for your passport, please contact the VFS center where your application has been submitted or the Belgian Embassy/Consulate General via email to request a temporary withdrawal of your passport.
For more information on the processing time, please visit the website of the Belgian Ministry of Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/en).

## Download Form
Please click here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=/en) to fill in the online application form

visa_types.name: Short Stay
visa_types.overview:
Short-Stay visa (C type) entitles its holder to travel throughout the 26 Schengen States for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Visas for visits exceeding that period remain subject to national procedures.Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/77r9SPyx2uDhSOHbkccn6K/9bfedee85a8e483540e3eb9ae30e7655/Schengen-Visa.pdf) to know more about Schengen Visa. Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her  family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her  country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.) 
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
In  case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa  application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However,  If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a  Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly  note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be  present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new  biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the  right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
- Business / Conference / Fair (#business/conference/fair)
- Tourism (#tourism)
- Family and Friends Visit (#familyandfriendsvisit)
- Professional Training (#professionaltraining)
visa_types.full_text:
Short-Stay visa (C type) entitles its holder to travel throughout the 26 Schengen States for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Visas for visits exceeding that period remain subject to national procedures.Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/77r9SPyx2uDhSOHbkccn6K/9bfedee85a8e483540e3eb9ae30e7655/Schengen-Visa.pdf) to know more about Schengen Visa. Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her  family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her  country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.) 
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
In  case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa  application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However,  If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a  Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly  note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be  present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new  biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the  right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
- Business / Conference / Fair (#business/conference/fair)
- Tourism (#tourism)
- Family and Friends Visit (#familyandfriendsvisit)
- Professional Training (#professionaltraining)
